Here's a paragraph about "Medicago sativa" (Lucerne or Alfalfa):

Medicago sativa, commonly known as Lucerne or Alfalfa, is a perennial legume with edible leaves, stems, and seeds. The health benefits of Medicago sativa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support bone density due to its rich content of isoflavones, saponins, and other bioactive compounds. The bioactive constituents of Medicago sativa include isoflavones (e.g., genistein and formononetin), saponins (e.g., medicagenin and sativoside), and flavonoids (e.g., kaempferol and quercetin). The most common herbal preparations made with Medicago sativa include teas, capsules, and tablets that support joint health, immune function, and overall well-being.
